one_liner: Google Ads API provides broad REST/gRPC access to campaign management and reporting via OAuth2 + developer-token auth, free to use but gated by a manager-account developer token application and access-level approval.

auth_methods.notes: Requires OAuth 2.0 access token plus a developer-token header; supports service account, single-user, and multi-user OAuth workflows with scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/adwords. login-customer-id header required when using a manager account.

self_serve_notes: API usage itself is free, but developers must obtain a developer token via a Google Ads manager account and apply for an access level (Basic/Standard) through the API Center; this involves a Google review/approval process (Required Minimum Functionality compliance) rather than instant self-serve API key generation.

api_surface.notes: Also gRPC/client libraries (Java, Python, etc.); GAQL query language for reporting; broad coverage of campaign, ad group, keyword, budget, and reporting resources.

main_blocker: Developer token approval process (manager account setup + access level application) adds friction before an agent toolkit can call the live API; official MCP server is read-only, so write-capable workflows require community forks or custom implementation.

evidence.supports: Developer token required, obtained via manager account
Access levels and RMF approval process for API access
API is free to use at all access levels
